description: The Five Points Gang was a criminal street gang based in the Five Points of Lower Manhattan, New York City, during the late 19th and early 20th century. Founded by Paul Kelly (born Paolo Antonio Vaccarelli), it initially consisted primarily of Irish-Americans but later became predominantly Italian-American. The gang engaged in racketeering, extortion, illegal gambling, and other crimes. Many of its members, including Johnny Torrio, Al Capone, and Lucky Luciano, later became prominent criminals.
